$comment: Shared endpoint/shape contract for the synthetic monitor (docs/synthetic-monitor-design.md). Consumed by monitoring/probe.py in BOTH production mode and the probe-contract CI job — this file is the one place to update when a user journey's endpoint or response shape changes. Placeholders: {spot} = referenceSpot, {today} = spot-local today (YYYY-MM-DD).

recencyAnchor.$comment: Probe-owned, backend-distrusting anchor: generatedAt must be < maxAgeHours. The constant lives HERE (physics/UX-derived), never read from backend config. Gated by PROBE_ENABLE_T4_ANCHOR until /health/summary + generatedAt are deployed.

tier1.name: region intel (key-presence only)
tier1.$comment: Key-presence only: a sportfishingreport scrape outage must NOT page. hotSpecies is @Deprecated; speciesWithTrends is current but omitted from JSON when empty (encodeDefaults=false), so assert on always-present keys and demote data quality to W7. In local mode (no seeded reports) the route legitimately 404s with its distinctive error body — accepted as proof the route exists, unlike Ktor's empty-body unknown-route 404.

tier2.name: GIBS tile from runner (app hits GIBS directly)
tide microservice
tier2.$comment: status=='ok' expected; a 200 with status=='loading' (warm instance cold-starting) is a warn-tier observation, never incident material.

tier2.name: intel data quality incl. AI insights (reuses T7 response)

deferred.$comment: W4 (Copernicus WMTS layer rotation) and W5 (sportfishingreport HTML drift) are deferred per the design doc's minimal-subset note: bot-hostile upstreams from Azure runners were the old probe's noise source. Add here with vantageBlockedSuppression when wanted.
